title: Fermented British Roast
description: A rich, tender, and flavorful dish featuring a shio-koji cure, dark brown sugar, and a halal-friendly twist. Perfect for a crowd-pleaser dinner.
instructions: Combine shio-koji, dark brown sugar, sea salt, black pepper, garlic, ginger, onion, carrot, celery, and bay leaves in a blender or food processor and blend until well combined.
Rinse the chuck roast under cold running water and pat dry with paper towels.
Rub the shio-koji cure all over the roast, making sure to coat it evenly.
Place the roast in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot and add the apple cider vinegar, beef broth, water, and thyme.
Bring the liquid to a boil over high heat, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the roast for 2 1/2 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 160°F (71°C).
Remove the roast from the oven and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.
Strain the braising liquid and discard the solids.
Skim off any excess fat and serve the roast with the braising liquid spooned over the top.
